expectations.text: Explains Bitnami stores auto-generated passwords in a Secret and reads from it on upgrade
expectations.evidence: 'auto-generate passwords on first install and store them in a Secret ... reads the password from that Secret'

expectations.text: Identifies auth.postgresPassword in values is silently ignored on upgrade
expectations.evidence: 'the auth.postgresPassword in your values file is silently ignored'

expectations.text: Provides kubectl get secret ... base64 -d retrieval command
expectations.evidence: 'kubectl get secret postgres0 -n db -o jsonpath="{.data.postgres-password}" | base64 -d'

expectations.text: Shows corrected upgrade passing password via --set auth.postgresPassword
expectations.evidence: '--set auth.postgresPassword="$POSTGRES_PASSWORD"'

expectations.text: Avoids suggesting re-setting the password in values fixes it
expectations.evidence: 'Setting or re-setting auth.postgresPassword in the values file will not fix this'

expectations.text: Explains Bitnami stores auto-generated passwords in a Secret and reads from it on upgrade
expectations.evidence: 'materializes the credential into a Secret ... reuses the value already stored there'

expectations.text: Identifies auth.postgresPassword in values is silently ignored on upgrade
expectations.evidence: 'silently ignoring whatever you put in auth.postgresPassword'

expectations.text: Provides kubectl get secret ... base64 -d retrieval command
expectations.evidence: 'kubectl get secret postgres0 -n db -o jsonpath={.data.postgres-password} | base64 -d'

expectations.text: Shows corrected upgrade passing password via --set auth.postgresPassword
expectations.evidence: '--set auth.postgresPassword="$POSTGRES_PASSWORD"'

expectations.text: Avoids suggesting re-setting the password in values fixes it
expectations.evidence: 'Re-typing the password in the values file does not fix this'

expectations.text: States --atomic on a failed install deletes the release entirely
expectations.evidence: 'rollback means Helm deletes the release entirely — there is no revision 1 left behind'

expectations.text: Instructs helm install (not upgrade) because the release was deleted
expectations.evidence: 'Re-run helm install, not helm upgrade ... the release no longer exists'

expectations.text: Flags the command missing --version and why pinning is required
expectations.evidence: 'your example was missing --version. Every helm install and upgrade must pin the chart version'

expectations.text: Explains omitting --version pulls latest, which may restructure/rename values
expectations.evidence: 'omitting it silently pulls whatever the latest chart version ... rename or move keys'

expectations.text: States --atomic on a failed install deletes the release entirely
expectations.evidence: 'no prior successful revision to roll back to, it deletes the release entirely'

expectations.text: Instructs helm install (not upgrade) because the release was deleted
expectations.evidence: 'helm install ... Because the release no longer exists, helm upgrade would fail'

expectations.text: Flags the command missing --version and why pinning is required
expectations.evidence: 'Your command is missing --version — this is a real bug'

expectations.text: Explains omitting --version pulls latest, which may restructure/rename values
expectations.evidence: 'silently resolves to the latest chart version ... renaming keys, moving fields under global'

expectations.text: Includes global.security.allowInsecureImages: true with Bitnami image-signing rationale
expectations.evidence: 'allowInsecureImages: true ... Bitnami charts enforce an image-signing / provenance check'

expectations.text: Formats imagePullSecrets as flat string list (- scaleway.registry)
expectations.evidence: 'imagePullSecrets:\n    - scaleway.registry # flat string list — NOT {name: ...}'

expectations.text: Sets image.registry/repository/tag as separate fields
expectations.evidence: 'registry: rg.fr-par.scw.cloud / repository: myorg/pgvector / tag: 17.3.0-debian-12-r0'

expectations.text: Pins the exact tag (17.3.0-debian-12-r0), not latest
expectations.evidence: 'tag: 17.3.0-debian-12-r0 # pin exact tag'

expectations.text: Avoids the k8s pod-spec imagePullSecrets format ({name: ...})
expectations.evidence: 'Do not use the Kubernetes pod-spec form {name: scaleway.registry} here'

expectations.text: Includes global.security.allowInsecureImages: true with Bitnami image-signing rationale
expectations.evidence: 'Bitnami enforces image verification ... opt out with global.security.allowInsecureImages: true'

expectations.text: Formats imagePullSecrets as flat string list (- scaleway.registry)
expectations.evidence: 'imagePullSecrets:\n    - scaleway.registry' ('flat list of strings ... not the pod-spec form')

expectations.text: Sets image.registry/repository/tag as separate fields
expectations.evidence: 'registry: rg.fr-par.scw.cloud / repository: myorg/pgvector / tag: 17.3.0-debian-12-r0'

expectations.text: Pins the exact tag (17.3.0-debian-12-r0), not latest
expectations.evidence: 'tag: 17.3.0-debian-12-r0 # pin exactly; never latest'

expectations.text: Avoids the k8s pod-spec imagePullSecrets format ({name: ...})
expectations.evidence: '# WRONG for Bitnami chart values: imagePullSecrets: - name: scaleway.registry'

notes: NON-DISCRIMINATING on all 3 evals: baseline scored 14/14, matching with_skill on every Bitnami/Helm gotcha (Secret-owned password on upgrade + kubectl retrieval; --atomic failed-install purge => helm install; --version pinning; allowInsecureImages; flat-string imagePullSecrets incl. explicit rejection of the {name: ...} pod-spec form). Base model already holds these operational gotchas.
